# Claude Code prompt — [5A.2] Quote PDF migration to the HTML renderer (PR 2 of §5A.2)

*Paste everything below the line into Claude Code. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-25.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow
it. This is **PR 2 of roadmap §5A.2** — migrate the **Quote PDF** from the
PDFKit builder to the HTML→PDF renderer shipped in PR #220, and **delete the
PDFKit quote builder**.

## Decisions already made — implement exactly as stated

- **Straight switch, delete PDFKit now.** This PR removes the PDFKit quote
  builder outright. No `PDF_ENGINE` flag, no fallback path. This is a
  deliberate, owner-approved override of the roadmap line "PDFKit code retired
  only after sign-off" — do not treat it as a mistake or reinstate a fallback.
- **Locate the reference template yourself.** Run `ls "C:\ProjectOperations-Reference\"`,
  identify the Quote reference document (a `.docx`/`.xlsx`), and build the HTML
  template to match its letterhead and layout. If more than one file could
  plausibly be the Quote reference, stop and ask before building.

## Pre-work (Phase 0)

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `feat/5a2-quote-pdf-html`.
- Conflict check on the files you plan to touch.
- **Read before writing:**
  - `apps/api/src/modules/estimate-export/pdf/quote-pdf.builder.ts` — the
    ~1,170-line PDFKit builder. It is the authority for *what the quote PDF
    must contain*: every section, the `QuoteOverlay` logic, simple/detailed
    modes, per-section visibility flags, cost-line visibility, provisional
    sums, cost options, signature block, watermark.
  - `apps/api/src/modules/client-quotes/quote-pdf.service.ts` and
    `apps/api/src/modules/estimate-export/estimate-export.service.ts` — the
    two consumers (see Scope).
  - `apps/api/src/modules/pdf-rendering/` — the renderer you will call.
  - The Quote reference document in `C:\ProjectOperations-Reference\`.

## Scope — TWO consumers, both must be migrated

`buildQuotePdf` (from `quote-pdf.builder.ts`) has **two** production consumers.
Migrate both — if you migrate only one, the other fails to compile when the
builder is deleted:

1. `QuotePdfService.generate()` — `client-quotes/quote-pdf.service.ts` — the
   per-`ClientQuote` PDF (`GET /clientquotes/:quoteId/pdf`), called **with** a
   `QuoteOverlay`. Also feeds the "send quote via Outlook" attachment path.
2. `EstimateExportService.exportPdf()` —
   `estimate-export/estimate-export.service.ts` — the tender-level quote PDF
   (`GET /tenders/:id/export/pdf`), called **without** an overlay.

The new HTML quote builder must handle both the overlay and no-overlay cases,
exactly as `buildQuotePdf(base, overlay?)` does today.

## What to build

1. **HTML/CSS quote template** under `apps/api/src/modules/pdf-rendering/templates/`
   (e.g. a `quote/` subdir). It must:
   - Match Sean's reference document for letterhead, layout, and visual design.
   - Reproduce every section the PDFKit builder produces: cover page, cost
     summary, scope table (simple vs detailed), assumptions, exclusions,
     provisional sums, cost options, referenced drawings, two-column T&C,
     signature/acceptance block, IS watermark + logo.
   - Honour the IS PDF spec (project_instructions §11): margins 15 mm L/R,
     25 mm top, 20 mm bottom; watermark per §11. Use the brand fonts **Outfit**
     (body) / **Syne** (headings) per §5 — the renderer already bundles them.
     (§11's "Helvetica" note was a PDFKit limitation and no longer applies.)
   - Honour every `QuoteOverlay` flag: `showProvisional`, `showCostOptions`,
     `showScopeTable`, `showAssumptions`, `showExclusions`,
     `showReferencedDrawings`, `detailLevel` (simple/detailed), per-cost-line
     `isVisible`, `assumptionMode`. Hidden lines/sections are excluded from
     output — never an error.

2. **An HTML quote builder** — a function/service that takes the same inputs
   as `buildQuotePdf` (`ExportPayload` + optional `QuoteOverlay`), assembles
   the full HTML document **programmatically** (the renderer's flat `{{key}}`
   interpolation is too limited for a document with repeated rows and
   conditional sections — build the HTML in code), and renders it via
   `PdfRendererService.renderHtmlToPdf` → `Buffer`.
   - **HTML-escape every dynamic value** — descriptions, client/contact names,
     notes, clause text, anything from the database. Unescaped data containing
     `<`, `&`, etc. will break layout or inject markup. Use a small escape
     helper.

3. **Rewire both consumers** to call the new HTML builder instead of
   `buildQuotePdf`. Keep their existing data-assembly logic (fetching, building
   the `QuoteOverlay`, the `EstimateExport` audit row, the filename) and keep
   their **public method signatures and return shapes** (`{ buffer, filename }`)
   unchanged, so the controllers and the Outlook-send path are unaffected.

4. **Delete** `apps/api/src/modules/estimate-export/pdf/quote-pdf.builder.ts`.

## Do NOT delete — keep these

- `apps/api/src/modules/estimate-export/pdf/tc-text.const.ts` — also imported
  by `estimate-export.service.ts`, `quote/quote.controller.ts`, and
  `quote/tc-parser.ts`. Keep it; the HTML builder may reuse its constants.
- The `pdfkit` / `@types/pdfkit` dependencies — `pdfkit` is still used by the
  persona drawing-tool **test specs** (`extract-drawing-titleblock`,
  `read-tender-drawing`, `read-asbestos-register` `.spec.ts`) to generate
  fixture PDFs. Verify, and keep the dependency.
- `estimate-export/excel/estimate-excel.builder.ts` — the Excel export,
  unrelated.

## Tests

- Update or replace every test that referenced `buildQuotePdf` —
  `estimate-export.service.spec.ts` and any quote-pdf tests — since
  `buildQuotePdf` no longer exists.
- Add an integration test that renders a quote PDF through the new path for
  **both** the overlay and no-overlay cases, and verifies a valid PDF with the
  expected page count (`pdfjs-dist`) and that the key sections/content are
  present.
- The full §6 pre-PR CI checklist must pass.

## Sign-off artifact

Generate a sample rendered quote PDF from the seeded IS-T020 demo tender and
its `ClientQuote`, and save it to a path under the repo (it does not need to be
committed) so Sean can review visual fidelity against his reference and Cowork
can smoke-test it. State the path in the PR body.

## Azure runtime

This is the first time the renderer runs a real document, so the deployed
Azure Web App must have Chromium available. The `onlyBuiltDependencies` change
from PR #220 makes `pnpm install` provision Chromium; confirm the deploy
workflow carries it into the artifact and that the runtime has Chromium's
system libraries. If the deployed app cannot launch Chromium, the quote PDF
endpoints will 500 in production — resolve it as part of this PR.

## Documentation (same PR, §6)

- `progress.md` — merge entry.
- `roadmap.md` — mark the §5A.2 Quote-PDF item ✅; changelog entry. Leave
  Variation and Schedule-of-Rates 🔲.
- `project_instructions.md` — update where it describes quote PDF generation
  (§6; §11's PDFKit-specific notes; §13 if relevant). Add a short note that
  pnpm 10 ignores dependency build scripts, so binary-downloading deps (e.g.
  `puppeteer`) must be allow-listed in `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies`.

- Title: `[5A.2] Quote PDF — HTML template + migration`
- Body: summary; files added / modified / **deleted** (`quote-pdf.builder.ts`);
  both consumers migrated; the sample-PDF path for Sean's sign-off; no new
  dependencies; no new env vars; no migrations; §6 checklist ticked. Record

## Token budget

Standard rule — if you approach the context limit, finish the current file,
run lint, commit `wip: feat/5a2-quote-pdf-html — pausing for token reset`,
push, and stop. Resume and complete everything before opening the PR.
